description_long: This is a large, multi-story suburban house with a complex, asymmetrical layout and a dark gray gable roof. The exterior walls are finished in a light beige or gray siding, and the house features numerous rectangular windows arranged in both single and grouped patterns. A prominent feature is the backyard area, which includes a small, white gazebo and a fenced patio space. The house sits on a slightly raised, grassy plot, with the terrain sloping gently away from the structure. Architectural details include a mix of roof heights, a projecting side wing, and a modest entryway with steps leading up to the main door.
description: A large, multi-story suburban house with a dark gable roof, light siding, many windows, and a backyard gazebo on a grassy lot.
description_view_0: Side view showing the projecting wing, grassy yard, and partial view of the backyard gazebo.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the long roofline, multiple windows, and the attached side structure.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with a clear look at the gazebo, fenced patio, and the house's vertical massing.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the main entryway, symmetrical window arrangement, and the full extent of the backyard area.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement with grouped rectangular windows
attribute_entryway_design: Simple main entry with steps, modest overhang, and no prominent porch or columns
